national_mail_in: see gold-domains buyers.ts — scored tier, available in all metros

buyers.name: Bulow Jewelers
buyers.payout_claims: "Pays up to 80% of melt value for gold jewelry" — described as "nearly double what you'd get from typical 'cash for gold' buyers"
buyers.testing_transparency: Site says "We test and weigh your gold in front of you, explaining the process every step of the way."
buyers.evidence_notes: Downtown Denver jeweler (16th Street Mall tower suite) that publishes an actual payout ceiling — rare among locals; drop-ins welcome, appointments optional via form or phone.

buyers.name: Gold Rush Denver
buyers.address: 2370 South Colorado Blvd, Denver, CO 80222 (7 Front Range locations incl. Aurora, Lakewood, Littleton, Westminster, Centennial, Colorado Springs)
buyers.payout_claims: "We have the highest payouts"; cash paid "usually, in less than 30 minutes" — no numbers posted
buyers.testing_transparency: Site describes an industry-standard multi-step process using cutting-edge x-ray assay technology at select locations to assess precious metals in unusual forms.
buyers.evidence_notes: Multi-location dedicated buyer chain; homepage names XRF as its testing technology; displays BBB A+, GIA, RapNet, AGA badges; free evaluation, 'No Pressure & No Obligation'; walk-ins accepted.

buyers.name: Rocky Mountain Coin
buyers.address: 538 S Broadway, Denver, CO 80209 (second store: 9625 E Arapahoe Rd Ste C, Greenwood Village, CO 80112)
buyers.hours: Denver: Mon–Fri 9:30am–2pm; Greenwood Village: Mon–Fri 10am–4pm; both closed weekends
buyers.payout_claims: "Buy and sell daily at the most competitive prices" — no numbers posted
buyers.testing_transparency: Testing method not described on site.
buyers.evidence_notes: Self-described "Colorado's Largest and Oldest Rare Coin and Precious Metals Dealer," in business since 1976 with two metro stores; strong coin/bullion focus; limited weekday-only hours worth noting.

buyers.name: Colorado Gold Mart
buyers.payout_claims: "We will give you cash on the spot or you can trade for something else we have in our shop" — no numbers posted
buyers.testing_transparency: Site says "Your gold will be evaluated and tested for content in front of you" — method not named; free appraisal, no pressure to sell.
buyers.evidence_notes: Gold buyer, vintage-watch dealer, and jewelry repair shop 'in the business for over 20 years'; free-appraisal-and-walk-away policy stated plainly.

buyers.name: Dave's Gold & Silver Exchange
buyers.payout_claims: "Get paid top value for your items!" — no numbers posted
buyers.testing_transparency: Testing method not described on site; assessments offered "completely free of charge."
buyers.evidence_notes: Family-run exchange (search snippets say since 1988) on Denver's Broadway antique row, two doors from Rocky Mountain Coin; appointment-led ('Schedule your free, no-obligation assessment'); very short posted hours — confirm before sending anyone.

buyers.name: Colorado Precious Metals Exchange (CPMEX)
buyers.address: 3160 Village Vista Dr Unit 101, Erie, CO 80516
buyers.payout_claims: "We pay more for numismatic bullion material or premium brand gold jewelry"; references "live gold payouts" — no percentages posted
buyers.testing_transparency: Denver-gold-buyers page says experts "evaluate and scrutinize every piece of gold"; company marketing elsewhere names x-ray fluorescence spectroscopy testing.
buyers.evidence_notes: Coin/bullion dealer marketing itself as 'the premier gold buyer, dealer, and coin shop in Denver' but physically located in Erie (north metro); search snippet cites XRF testing; walk-in or appointment. Distance from Denver proper worth flagging to users.
